metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state in PostgreSQL (84 TypeORM entities), exposes full CRUD operations on business entities (apps, users, organizations, data sources, workflows), and manages entity lifecycle with status fields and versioning.

readiness_profile.deployment_gate: Resolve all blockers before any agent deployment — including pilots.

findings.title: No machine identity authentication for agents
findings.description: The application supports JWT-based authentication via PassportJS with session management designed for human users. There is no support for service account or machine identity authentication (OAuth 2.0 client credentials, API key with principal attribution, or mTLS).
findings.gap: No dedicated machine identity authentication mechanism exists. An agent cannot authenticate as an independent principal separate from a human user.
findings.recommendation: Implement an API key authentication strategy that creates independent machine principals with attribution logged in audit entries.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Implement an API key authentication strategy that creates independent machine principals (not tied to human user accounts) with attribution logged in audit entries.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Agents authenticate via OAuth 2.0 client credentials or dedicated API keys, each mapped to a unique machine principal visible in audit logs.

findings.title: Coarse-grained permissions lack resource-instance scoping
findings.description: CASL-based authorization supports per-feature, per-role permissions but is coarse-grained at feature level, not resource-instance level. No mechanism to grant an agent read-only access to specific apps or data sources.
findings.gap: No resource-instance-level permission scoping. An agent identity with 'view apps' permission sees all apps in the organization.
findings.recommendation: Implement resource-instance-level permissions in the CASL ability factory to allow agent identities to be scoped to specific app IDs or data source IDs.

compensating_controls: Assign agent identities the most restrictive role (End User) with custom group permissions limited to specific features
Create a dedicated organization/workspace for agent-accessible resources to limit blast radius

findings.title: Action-level authorization coupled to human role hierarchy
findings.description: CASL authorization enforces action-level checks at the controller level. The AbilityGuard validates permissions before allowing route access. However, action-level auth is coupled to human role hierarchy and not independently configurable per agent identity.
findings.gap: Action-level authorization exists but cannot be independently configured per machine identity.
findings.recommendation: Extend the CASL ability factory to support per-principal action overrides, allowing machine identities to have custom action restrictions independent of the role hierarchy.

compensating_controls: Use the End User role (most restricted action set) for any agent identity
Implement API Gateway-level method restrictions to limit which HTTP methods an agent token can invoke

findings.title: Audit logs stored in mutable database without immutability guarantees
findings.description: The application has a ResponseInterceptor that emits audit log events captured in an audit_logs table. However, audit log storage is in the application's own PostgreSQL database — not immutable, not tamper-evident, and has no log integrity validation.
findings.gap: Audit logs stored in mutable application database with no tamper-evidence or immutability guarantees. No external immutable log sink configured.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with CloudTrail or configure an S3 bucket with object lock to receive audit log streams. Add a principal_type field to distinguish human vs. machine actions.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, so audit logging is important but not a deployment blocker.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, reducing audit-trail urgency from BLOCKER to RISK-SAFETY.
compensating_controls: Ship audit logs to an external immutable store (S3 with object lock, CloudTrail Data Events)
Configure database-level write-once protections on the audit_logs table

findings.title: No independent agent identity suspension mechanism
findings.description: User accounts can be deactivated and sessions invalidated. However, there is no dedicated mechanism to suspend a specific agent/machine identity independently since no machine identity concept exists.
findings.gap: No independent agent identity suspension mechanism. Suspending an agent would require disabling the human user account.
findings.recommendation: Once machine identities exist (AUTH-Q1), add an immediate suspension endpoint that revokes all active sessions for a specific machine principal.

compensating_controls: Implement session-level revocation at the API Gateway layer
Use short-lived tokens for agent access so natural expiry limits exposure

findings.title: No circuit breaker protection for external dependency calls
findings.description: No circuit breaker library is used. The only retry logic is a hand-coded 3-attempt retry for PostgREST schema reconfiguration. The application makes external calls to 47+ data source connectors without circuit breaker protection.
findings.gap: No circuit breaker pattern for external dependency calls. A failing data source connector could cascade failures back to calling agents.
findings.recommendation: Add a circuit breaker library (opossum for Node.js) around external data source calls. Configure per-connector timeout and failure thresholds.

compensating_controls: Configure API Gateway-level timeout limits on agent-facing endpoints
Implement data source query timeout limits at the application level

findings.title: No global API rate limiting
findings.description: Only the Workflows module has rate limiting (100 requests per 60 seconds via @nestjs/throttler on webhook endpoints). There is no global rate limiting on the API.
findings.gap: No global API rate limiting. An agent calling endpoints at machine speed would face no throttling.
findings.recommendation: Apply @nestjs/throttler globally with sensible defaults, and configure API Gateway throttling in the Terraform ECS configuration.

compensating_controls: Deploy an API Gateway or load balancer with rate limiting in front of the application
Configure WAF rate rules at the infrastructure layer

findings.title: No PII redaction in logging pipeline
findings.description: The application uses Pino for logging with request context. There is no PII redaction middleware — no log scrubbing, no field masking, no Macie integration. User emails, names, and potentially sensitive data source connection strings could appear in logs.
findings.gap: No PII redaction in logging pipeline. User-identifying information and potentially sensitive data source credentials may appear in application logs and traces.
findings.recommendation: Implement Pino redaction paths for sensitive fields (password, email, connectionString, apiKey) and add a log sanitization middleware.

compensating_controls: Configure Pino serializers to redact sensitive fields
Apply CloudWatch log filters to mask PII patterns before storage

findings.title: No machine-readable API specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, Swagger, AsyncAPI, or GraphQL schema files exist. API is defined implicitly through NestJS controller decorators across 60+ modules.
findings.gap: No machine-readable API specification for agent tool generation.
findings.recommendation: Add @nestjs/swagger integration and annotate controllers with API decorators to auto-generate an OpenAPI specification.

compensating_controls: Use @nestjs/swagger decorators on existing controllers to auto-generate an OpenAPI spec
Manually document the most critical agent-facing endpoints as a bridge

findings.title: Error responses lack retryable classification
findings.description: Global AllExceptionsFilter produces structured JSON errors with statusCode, timestamp, path, message, code. Missing a retryable boolean or error category field.
findings.gap: Error responses lack a retryable field. Agents cannot programmatically determine whether to retry without parsing message strings.
findings.recommendation: Extend AllExceptionsFilter to include an isRetryable boolean and an errorCategory field.

compensating_controls: Document which HTTP status codes are retriable vs terminal for agent consumption
Add error category to the response filter output

findings.title: No API versioning or breaking change detection
findings.description: 184 TypeORM schema migrations provide database schema versioning. However, there is no API versioning, no breaking change detection in CI, and no consumer-driven contract tests.
findings.gap: No API versioning strategy. No breaking change detection tooling in CI pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Implement URL-based API versioning and add schema comparison tooling to the CI pipeline.

compensating_controls: Pin agent tool definitions to specific endpoint paths and validate responses in agent tests
Add OpenAPI diff checks in CI once a spec is generated

findings.title: Distributed tracing only available in Enterprise edition
findings.description: OpenTelemetry SDK is integrated but only for Enterprise/Cloud editions. CE edition has no distributed tracing. Pino provides structured JSON logging with correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Distributed tracing is available only in Enterprise/Cloud editions. CE deployment has no trace ID propagation.
findings.recommendation: Make OpenTelemetry tracing available in the CE edition.

compensating_controls: Enable OTEL in CE deployments by configuring the tracing module as an optional plugin
Use Pino transaction IDs as a correlation mechanism for single-service debugging

findings.title: No alerting configuration for API error rates or latency
findings.description: No alerting configuration found. Terraform ECS has CloudWatch log groups and Container Insights but no alarms. Sentry is integrated for error tracking but not latency alerting.
findings.gap: No configured alerting thresholds for API error rates or latency.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for ALB 5xx rates, p99 latency, and unhealthy host count.

compensating_controls: Configure CloudWatch alarms on ALB target group error rates in Terraform
Use Sentry performance monitoring alerts as a bridge

findings.title: IaC exists but lacks drift detection and enforced peer review
findings.description: Infrastructure is defined as code across 24 Terraform files, Helm charts, and Kubernetes manifests. No drift detection and no evidence of mandatory peer review on IaC changes.
findings.gap: IaC exists but lacks two of three governance controls: no drift detection and no enforced peer review for infrastructure changes.
findings.recommendation: Add CODEOWNERS rules for IaC directories, require terraform plan in CI, and configure AWS Config rules for drift detection.

compensating_controls: Add a CODEOWNERS file requiring IaC team review for terraform/ and deploy/ directories
Add terraform plan as a CI check on PRs that modify IaC files

findings.title: No API contract testing in CI pipeline
findings.description: CI pipeline includes build, lint, unit tests (Jest), and E2E tests (Cypress). No API contract tests, no Pact, no OpenAPI validation, no schema comparison tools.
findings.gap: No API contract testing in CI. Breaking changes to API endpoints would not be caught before production.
findings.recommendation: Add consumer-driven contract tests or OpenAPI schema validation in CI.

compensating_controls: Add Cypress API-level tests that validate response schemas
Implement OpenAPI spec generation + diff as part of CI

findings.title: No automated rollback triggers
findings.description: Deployments use Docker image tagging and kubectl set image. Helm chart supports standard rollback. No automated rollback trigger on health check failure.
findings.gap: No automated rollback trigger. Rollback is possible but requires manual intervention.
findings.recommendation: Configure automated rollback triggers via Kubernetes rolling update with readiness probes or Helm rollback on failed health checks.

compensating_controls: Use Helm rollback commands in incident response runbooks
Configure Kubernetes rollout undo triggers on readiness probe failures

findings.title: No infrastructure-level encryption at rest configured
findings.description: Terraform ECS configuration does not specify encryption at rest for RDS. LOCKBOX_MASTER_KEY provides application-level encryption for data source credentials only.
findings.gap: No infrastructure-level encryption at rest. Database storage unencrypted by default.
findings.recommendation: Add storage_encrypted = true and kms_key_id to RDS configuration. Enable encryption at rest on ElastiCache Redis.

compensating_controls: Enable RDS encryption at rest when provisioning
Ensure Redis ElastiCache has encryption at rest enabled

findings.title: Compensation and Rollback
findings.description: No compensation or rollback patterns for multi-step operations. Workflow status tracking exists but no compensating transactions.
findings.gap: No rollback capability for multi-step application operations.
findings.recommendation: Implement saga patterns or compensation logic for critical multi-step workflows before enabling write-scope agents.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: STATE-Q1 is a conditional BLOCKER. With read-only scope, it resolves to RISK-SAFETY because compensation capability is relevant for system maturity.
compensating_controls: Limit agent scope to read-only operations until compensation patterns are implemented
Implement human-in-the-loop approval for any multi-step workflows

findings.title: Data Residency and Sovereignty
findings.description: No explicit data residency configuration in the codebase. Self-hosted deployment means operator controls data location.
findings.gap: No application-level data residency enforcement or documentation.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency considerations for operators deploying in regulated environments.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q2 is a conditional BLOCKER. With read-only scope, it resolves to RISK-SAFETY because data residency is still relevant for read-only agents.
compensating_controls: Ensure LLM/agent endpoints are deployed in the same region as the ToolJet instance
Document data residency requirements for operators deploying in regulated environments
